However, I can provide information on a similar-sounding plant, "Belamcanda chinensis" (also known as Blackberry lily or Belamcanda), which is a different plant species.

If you are referring to "Belamcanda chinensis", here is a paragraph about it:

Belamcanda chinensis is a perennial plant species native to Asia, characterized by its brightly colored flowers and edible rhizomes. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammatory diseases, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Belamcanda chinensis include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used to make tea and powder for medicinal purposes.
